subject: Databases
title: LIMIT and OFFSET for Paging
description: A 60-second lesson on LIMIT and OFFSET for Paging.

hook.text: A blog has 10,000 posts. Show 20 per page. LIMIT 20 OFFSET 40 gives you page three.

buildup.text: LIMIT caps how many rows to return. OFFSET skips rows. Together they implement pagination.

discovery.text: Page 1: OFFSET 0. Page 2: OFFSET 20. Page 3: OFFSET 40. Simple math.

twist.text: OFFSET 100000 is slow. The database still reads and discards 100,000 rows. Cursor-based paging is faster.

climax.text: Cursor paging: WHERE id > last_seen_id LIMIT 20. Fast at any depth. No rows skipped.

punchline.text: OFFSET works for small pages. Cursors scale better.

quiz.question: Why is OFFSET-based pagination slow at high page numbers?
options: The database reads and discards all skipped rows before returning the result
OFFSET only works for the first 100 pages
High page numbers corrupt the index
